program.name: Atlas Platform Launch
program.description: Fictional B2B SaaS launch program — the hybrid bridge demo. One program, three projects spanning agile, waterfall, and hybrid delivery.

risks.title: Cross-team dependency stalls the critical path
risks.description: Platform Core API delays ripple into Migration and then the public launch.

risks.title: Program budget pressure
risks.description: A contractor rate increase threatens the back half of the program.

risks.title: Regulatory sign-off gates billing + cutover
risks.description: A compliance review must clear before billing go-live and the production cutover.

risks.title: Third-party security audit findings on SSO
Executive sponsor transition mid-program
risks.notes: New sponsor onboarded; launch funding reconfirmed.

projects.name: Platform Core
projects.description: The agile build stream — the platform itself, delivered sprint by sprint.

tasks.name: Tenant data cutover hook
tasks.notes: Committed to the active sprint but gated on the migration team's Performance tuning task — a cross-project predecessor whose finish lands after this sprint closes. Demonstrates the dependency-reality at-risk indicator (#372).

sprints.name: Sprint 1
sprints.goal: Stand up identity and the tenant core.

sprints.name: Sprint 2
sprints.goal: Access control and the public API surface.

sprints.name: Sprint 3
sprints.goal: Data platform foundations and the billing catalog.

sprints.name: Sprint 5
sprints.goal: Audit trail, dunning, and the warehouse backfill.

risks.title: Velocity dip after team expansion
risks.description: Onboarding three new engineers mid-program may dip velocity for 2 sprints.

risks.title: Billing scope creep
risks.description: Tax-engine requirements are still firming up and may expand the billing epic.

risks.title: SSO/identity vendor integration risk
risks.description: The chosen IdP's enterprise SSO certification is on the critical path.

risks.title: Notification provider rate limits
risks.notes: Batching + backoff implemented; load test passed.

projects.name: Migration Tooling
projects.description: The waterfall stream — CPM-scheduled data migration with three-point estimates.

risks.title: Legacy data quality worse than profiled
risks.description: Undocumented nulls and encoding issues could expand the transform effort.

risks.title: Cutover window too short
risks.description: The approved freeze window may not absorb a failed first cutover.

risks.title: Migration throughput below target
risks.description: Delta-sync throughput may not keep pace with production write volume.

risks.title: No tested rollback for a failed cutover
risks.description: A failed production cutover without a rehearsed rollback strands the migration.

projects.name: GTM Readiness
projects.description: The hybrid stream — gated launch planning with agile enablement content.

risks.title: Launch slips past fiscal quarter
risks.description: A migration slip cascades to the public-launch milestone and misses the quarter.

risks.title: Analyst-briefing embargo conflict
risks.notes: Embargo dates aligned with the launch gate; closed.

events.body: Starting SSO — OIDC discovery and the login callback.
Omar's pulled onto the migration assessment — moving Tenant model to Diego.
SSO login works against the IdP sandbox. PR up for review.
Security review: the state param isn't validated on the callback — that's a login CSRF hole. Sending it back, and logging it as an audit finding.
Validated state and nonce on the callback and added a regression test. Re-review please.
Solid now. Approving.
API key rotation slipped behind the SSO rework — carrying it into Sprint 2.
Positioning is locked; the pricing review with Finance is the long pole now.
Schema transformer scaffolded; wiring the field-mapping table.
Transform passes on the sample extract. Ready for review.
Review: the account-type enum maps three legacy codes to one, which silently drops a distinction Finance relies on. Sending it back.
Split the enum mapping and added a reconciliation check. Re-review.
Reconciles now. Approved.
Billing sandbox access blocked both 3-pointers — carrying Credit memo flow and Notification templates into Sprint 4.
Cap review WIP at 2
Split stories larger than 8 points
Split stories larger than 8 points
Kicking off the dry-run against the full production extract.
Delta sync needs deeper data-platform knowledge — moving it from Raj to Omar.
Blocked: Migration's performance tuning now lands after this sprint closes — parking the cutover hook until the tuned pipeline exists.
Warehouse schema churn means we need an extra backfill pass this sprint — pulling Backfill tool into the sprint.
Launch blog drafted through the pricing section.
We'll protect the sprint goal by deferring lower-priority work. Accepting the injection — and it's bigger than we thought: re-pointing from 5 to 8.
Dunning flow ready for review — retry ladder and grace periods are in.
Dry-run is halfway through; the tenant shard fan-out is the hot spot.
Re-estimating performance tuning off the dry-run findings — 4 days was optimistic.
Onboarding guide is live in the docs hub.
Digest batching is in; the preference fan-out is what's left.
Draft launch blog is with brand review; hero image still open.
